items.title: Whyalla workers injured at steelworks' blast furnace

children.content: Three Whyalla steelworks employees have been treated for injuries suffered during attempts to restart the site's troubled blast furnace, the ABC understands.

gallery.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

thumbnail.altText: A worker at the face of the blast furnace at the Whyalla steelworks.

items.title: Inquest to determine whether man killed his mother and staged her death

children.content: An inquest is investigating whether a man killed his mother before placing her in the driver's seat of her car to stage that she was behind the wheel during a crash.

image.altText: A man with brown hair and beard smiles for the camera inside a family kitchen

items.title: Labor conference braces for gambling, gas and AI stoushes

children.content: Labor members and unions are gearing up for fights over gambling, gas and AI at the party's national conference later this week.

image.altText: A man's side profile in shadows with a phone close to his face

items.title: Rain cancels outback reunions and events for second year

children.content: Flood-damaged roads and soggy racetracks mean some of the outback's favourite events are being cancelled and keeping tourists away.

gallery.altText: A young girl riding a horse, with a woman leading the horse

thumbnail.altText: A young girl riding a horse, with a woman leading the horse

items.title: Minister says constitution limits ability to block return of IS-linked doctor

children.content: Murray Watt says the government won't be providing assistance to an Australian doctor with links to Islamic State jailed in an Iraqi prison. Follow live.

image.altText: Penny Wong and Wang Yi shake hands.

items.title: Duo sails toilet to Kangaroo Island on homemade barge

children.content: When two mates "sailed" a toilet barge across Backstairs Passage to Kangaroo Island, there was more to it than just having a laugh.

image.altText: Two men aboard a barge with an old fashioned toilet and a barbecue on it

items.title: Randa Abdel-Fattah appears to abandon threatened defamation case against SA Premier

children.content: Earlier this year, Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah said she had sent two concerns notices to SA Premier Peter Malinauskas over comments made around the time of her controversial removal from the line-up of this year's Adelaide Writers' Week.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair, wearing a black embroidered jacket, with her hands clasped together.

items.title: SA government to explore legal options against developer

children.content: The SA government says it is exploring all legal options to respond to developer Wel.Co after it walked away from 75 contracts at a site north of Adelaide.

image.altText: A man in a dark blazer and blue shirt

items.title: Tony Modra shares post-surgery photos after truck crash

children.content: AFL legend Tony Modra shares a post-crash update on social media, posting photos of him in hospital and with family, and saying he is now "getting out and about".

gallery.altText: Adelaide Crows legend Tony Modra with family.

thumbnail.altText: Adelaide Crows legend Tony Modra with family.

items.title: Glitter in 'beautiful big nugget of rhino poo' unlocks fertility clues

children.content: Edible glitter is being used to track the reproductive cycles of the two youngest southern white rhinos at Monarto Safari Park.

gallery.altText: A southern white rhino chewing on hay with vivid blue glitter.

thumbnail.altText: A southern white rhino chewing on hay with vivid blue glitter.

items.title: South Australians urged to consider occasional power outages to curb costs

children.content: The driving force behind skyrocketing power prices this century has been investment by grid owners, and sometimes over-investment, but one expert believes it's time to start questioning our priorities if we want to reduce costs.

image.altText: Road to Garden Island boat ramp

items.title: Alleged murderer denies killing woman, says he knew 'something was wrong'

children.content: A man accused of murdering a woman in Adelaide's southern suburbs tells a court he is not to blame for her death, and instead alleges the crime was committed by the victim's ex-wife.

image.altText: A bearded man with a dog.

items.title: Plastic waste burning trial at cement factory prompts backlash

children.content: A major cement manufacturer has been allowed to burn more plastic waste for fuel at its Adelaide site in a decision a local council is seeking to challenge in court.

image.altText: A person outside the Adbri cement factory.

items.title: Tribunal upholds three-match ban for Crows forward Darcy Fogarty

children.content: The Crows fail in their challenge to Darcy Fogarty's three-match striking ban at the AFL Tribunal, as the panel finds Fogarty had time to decide to swing his arm at Sydney's Harry Cunningham. Follow live.

image.altText: An Adelaide Crows AFL forward points ahead in celebration after kicking a goal during a game.

items.title: Map reveals Australia's 'pharmacy deserts' are not all in the bush

children.content: Australia's "pharmacy deserts" have been mapped for the first time, revealing remote NT communities and outer-city Melbourne have fewer pharmacies per person than some other parts of the country.

gallery.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

thumbnail.altText: A female pharmacist is selecting a drug from the pharmacy inventory.

items.title: SA's largest electorate to be renamed in honour of Aboriginal leader

children.content: South Australia's largest federal electorate will be renamed in honour of an Aboriginal leader. Experts say it is also the state's most likely to fall to One Nation.

image.altText: a green voting slip is dropped into a voting box

items.title: Victorian teachers are going on strike again — here's what you need to know

children.content: Victorian state school teachers and educators are going ahead with their second 24-hour strike this year after union members rejected the government's latest pay offer.

image.altText: A dense crowd of people in red shirts with some holding signs walk through a CBD street.

items.title: 'We do not want to highlight this yet': AI data centre's FAQ slip-up

children.content: Data centre builder Firmus posted a statement, since removed, indicating its intention to get some of its cooling water from the state-owned irrigation system, adding "but we do not want to highlight this yet".

items.title: AI companies 'stole' Australian culture, news bosses say, as they call for licensing deals

image.altText: Hamish MacDonald,  Allesandra Galloni, Hugh Marks, Michael Miller, Jane Palfreyman, Matt Stanton.

children.content: The heads of the ABC, SBS, Nine and News Corp say AI companies should strike licensing deals rather than freely train on Australian news, warning journalism is a public good that must be protected.

items.title: Who paid for Pauline Hanson's trip to Europe? Not taxpayers, she says

items.title: 'Irresponsible': Russia conducts live-fire weapons exercise off British coast

items.title: Sixteen-year-old in custody suspected of killing mother, injuring little sister

items.title: Mother of identical quadruplets calls them her 'miracle babies'

children.content: A maternal foetal medicine expert believes identical quadruplets born in Brisbane may be even rarer than one in 15 million, given they shared a placenta.
